# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 8

This is turn 8. Turns 1 through 7 are consolidated in `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` or `current_manuscript.tex` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 8.

## Turn 8 — integration and canonicality sweep

I patched the attached stale source and compiled it successfully.

The revision now:

- deletes the false un-oriented Proposition 3.4 and all insertion-point/status language;
- proves the marked dyadic normalization directly via
  \[
  \operatorname{Aut}(D_0)\twoheadrightarrow
  \operatorname{Aut}(D_0^{\mathrm{ab}},\chi_0),
  \]
  using cyclotomic peripheral powering followed by the square-root/HNN lift;
- adds the local orientation row;
- replaces Lemma 6.3 by the seven-term-sequence uniqueness theorem for the normalized determinant class;
- uses that uniqueness to justify norm-one invariance uniformly on both the candidate and local quadratic forms;
- corrects Lemma 9.3: \(R\) is elementary abelian and centralized by \(K\), so its action factors through \(C=Y/K\); only invariant scalar pushouts are central;
- leaves the candidate wild relation unchanged.

The resulting PDF is 40 pages and compiles without undefined references, LaTeX warnings, or overfull boxes.

[Patched LaTeX](sandbox:/mnt/data/current_manuscript_turn8_patched.tex) · [Compiled PDF](sandbox:/mnt/data/current_manuscript_turn8_patched.pdf) · [Unified patch](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn8_normalization_and_canonicality.patch)

ESTABLISHED — The Turn 6 normalization and Turn 7 canonicality/centrality repairs are integrated, with the same structural uniqueness argument also closing the previously implicit unitary-invariance step in the candidate determinant calculation.

GAP — No new internal algebraic or dependency gap was found in this sweep; the highest-risk unaudited block is now the orbit-transfer/Evens-norm calculation in Lemmas 6.2, 6.11, and 6.12.

NEXT — Perform a line-by-line independent reconstruction of that orbit-transfer block, especially the involution-stabilized half-orbit and deep-unit Evens norm, before freezing the proof.

[INTEGRATED-CANONICALITY]
